heading: Free Clusters
body:
Atlas automatically stops collecting monitoring information
for a Free cluster after a few days of inactivity.

If there is no activity for 30 days, then Atlas automatically
pauses the cluster completely, disallowing any connections to it
until you resume the cluster. Atlas sends an email seven days
before pausing the cluster. Atlas sends another email after
pausing the cluster.

You can [resume](resume-cluster) the cluster at any time
*unless* the paused cluster was running on an old
version that Atlas can't restore to the current version. You
should export a copy of your data if you don't plan to use your
Free cluster for an extended period of time. To learn more, see
[Restore Data From Paused Free Cluster](restore-free-tier-cluster).

You can't initiate a pause for Free clusters.
